student_permit.title: Study Permit (Canada)
student_permit.overview: A study permit allows you to study at a designated learning institution (DLI) in Canada.

renewal.title: Renewing Your Study Permit
renewal.apply_by: Apply at least 30 days before expiry. If you apply before expiry you get implied status.

steps.deadline: ASAP — must apply 30 days before expiry
steps.details: Find the date on your study permit card. Do not wait — processing takes 60-90 days.
Request a letter from your university's registrar office confirming your enrollment status and program end date.
You need: valid passport, current study permit, enrollment letter, proof of financial support ($10,000+/year), passport photos, and your transcript.
Create or log into your IRCC account. Pay the $150 CAD fee. Submit all documents electronically. You get 'implied status' automatically upon submission.
Check your IRCC account weekly. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information.

important_notes: While on implied status, you can continue studying and working under same conditions as your previous permit
You cannot leave Canada while on implied status — you would lose it
Off-campus work (20h/week) requires a Social Insurance Number (SIN) — apply at Service Canada

work_while_studying.on_campus: Up to unlimited hours on campus — no work permit needed
work_while_studying.off_campus: Up to 20 hours/week during school year, full-time during breaks
work_while_studying.requirements: Must be enrolled full-time at a DLI, permit must say you can work off-campus

post_graduation.title: Post-Graduation Work Permit (PGWP)
post_graduation.overview: Allows you to work in Canada after graduating. Valid for up to 3 years depending on program length.
post_graduation.eligibility: Graduated from a 2+ year DLI program
post_graduation.duration: Equal to your program length (max 3 years for 2+ year programs)
post_graduation.apply_by: Within 180 days of getting your final marks

steps.action: Confirm your graduation and get official transcripts
steps.details: Request your degree/diploma and official transcripts from your institution.
Apply through IRCC. Fee: $255 CAD. Include transcripts, final letter of completion, passport, current study permit.

steps.action: Apply for Social Insurance Number (SIN) if not done
steps.details: Visit any Service Canada location. Free. Takes 1-2 days.

permanent_residency.title: Permanent Residency Pathways
permanent_residency.overview: Canada offers several pathways to permanent residency (PR) for international students and skilled workers.

pathways.name: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker)
pathways.description: Points-based system for skilled workers. Eligible after getting Canadian work experience.
requirements: 1+ year Canadian work experience OR foreign experience

pathways.name: Quebec Experience Program (PEQ)
pathways.description: Fastest PR pathway if you studied or worked in Quebec. Streamlined for Quebec graduates.
requirements: Diploma from Quebec institution OR 12+ months Quebec work experience

pathways.name: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) — Quebec
pathways.description: Quebec selects its own immigrants. Certificate of Selection (CSQ) is the key document.

credit_recognition.title: Credential Recognition for Engineers/Tech
steps: Request official transcript evaluation by WES Canada (World Education Services)
Submit diploma and transcripts to OIQ for review
Pass the exam on Quebec engineering practice (if required)
Register as junior engineer, accumulate supervised experience

key_contacts.name: Clinic Juridique du Mile-End (Free legal aid)
